---
title: "another_test"
output: html_document
date: "2025-05-04"
---
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
```

## Improved System for Remaining Useful Life Prediction

```{r load_libraries}
# Core data manipulation and analysis
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(readr)
library(zoo)
library(tidyverse)

# Visualization
library(ggplot2)
library(corrplot)
library(patchwork) # For combining plots

# Machine learning
library(caret)
library(randomForest)
library(xgboost)
library(glmnet)
library(e1071)
library(lightgbm)

# For parallel processing
library(parallel)
library(doParallel)

library(keras)
library(tensorflow)
library(survival)
library(survminer)
library(wavelets)
library(fftw)
library(tsfeatures)
library(iml)          # For model interpretation
library(lime)         # For local interpretable model-agnostic explanations
library(shapviz)      # For SHAP values
library(h2o)
```
```{r}
install.packages("h2o")

```
## Data Loading and Preparation

```{r data_loading}
# Define the structure of the NASA dataset
file_ids <- c("FD001", "FD002", "FD003", "FD004")
file_types <- c("train", "test", "RUL")

# Column names based on the NASA Turbofan dataset documentation
columns <- c('unit_number', 'time_in_cycles', 'setting_1', 'setting_2', 'setting_3', 
             'T2', 'T24', 'T30', 'T50', 'P2', 'P15', 'P30', 'Nf', 'Nc', 'epr', 
             'Ps30', 'phi', 'NRf', 'NRc', 'BPR', 'farB', 'htBleed', 'Nf_dmd', 
             'PCNfR_dmd', 'W31', 'W32')

# Improved function to load data
load_data <- function(file_id, file_type) {
  file_name <- paste0(file_type, "_", file_id, ".txt")
  
  if (!file.exists(file_name)) {
    warning(paste("File not found:", file_name))
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  tryCatch({
    # Read with readr for better performance
    data <- read_delim(file_name,
                     delim = " ",
                     col_names = FALSE,
                     trim_ws = TRUE,
                     na = c("", "NA", "NaN"),
                     skip_empty_rows = TRUE,
                     show_col_types = FALSE)  # Suppress column type messages
    
    # Remove empty columns (common in space-delimited files)
    data <- data[, colSums(!is.na(data)) > 0]
    
    # Apply column names if it's train or test file
    if (file_type %in% c("train", "test")) {
      # Make sure we don't run out of column names
      if (ncol(data) <= length(columns)) {
        colnames(data) <- columns[1:ncol(data)]
      } else {
        warning(paste("More columns than expected in", file_name))
        colnames(data) <- c(columns, paste0("unknown_", 1:(ncol(data) - length(columns))))
      }
    } else if (file_type == "RUL") {
      # For RUL files, use appropriate column name
      colnames(data) <- "RUL"
    }
    
    # Convert logical columns to numeric if necessary
    data <- mutate_if(data, is.logical, as.numeric)
    
    # Convert to tibble for better handling
    data <- as_tibble(data)
    return(data)
  }, error = function(e) {
    warning(paste("Error loading", file_name, ":", e$message))
    return(NULL)
  })
}

# Load all datasets with better error handling
data_list <- list()
for (file_id in file_ids) {
  for (file_type in c(file_types, "RUL")) {  # Include RUL files
    key <- paste0(file_type, "_", file_id)
    data <- load_data(file_id, file_type)
    if (!is.null(data)) {
      data_list[[key]] <- data
      cat(sprintf("Loaded %s: %d rows, %d columns\n", key, nrow(data), ncol(data)))
    }
  }
}

# Examine first few rows of one dataset to verify structure
head(data_list[["train_FD001"]])
```

## Data Preparation and RUL Calculation

```{r prepare_train_data}
# Enhanced function to prepare training data with proper RUL calculation
prepare_train_data <- function(data, min_cycles = 0) {
  data <- as_tibble(data)
  
  # Calculate RUL based on max cycles for each unit
  fd_RUL <- data %>%
    group_by(unit_number) %>%
    summarize(max_time = max(time_in_cycles), .groups = 'drop')
  
  data <- data %>%
    left_join(fd_RUL, by = "unit_number") %>%
    mutate(RUL = max_time - time_in_cycles) %>%
    select(-max_time)
  
  # Filter out early cycles if requested
  if (min_cycles > 0) {
    data <- data %>% filter(time_in_cycles >= min_cycles)
  }
  
  return(data)
}

# Function to prepare test data with actual RUL values
prepare_test_data <- function(test_data, rul_data) {
  # Get max cycle for each unit in test
  test_max_cycles <- test_data %>%
    group_by(unit_number) %>%
    summarize(max_time = max(time_in_cycles), .groups = 'drop')
  
  # Add RUL column from the RUL file 
  if (!is.null(rul_data)) {
    # Make sure rul_data has unit_number
    if (ncol(rul_data) == 1) {
      # If RUL data only has one column, assume it's the RUL values in order
      rul_with_unit <- tibble(
        unit_number = 1:nrow(rul_data),
        true_RUL = rul_data[[1]]
      )
    } else {
      # Otherwise assume first column is unit and second is RUL
      rul_with_unit <- tibble(
        unit_number = rul_data[[1]],
        true_RUL = rul_data[[2]]
      )
    }
    
    # Join with test units
    test_rul <- test_max_cycles %>%
      left_join(rul_with_unit, by = "unit_number")
    
    # Now add RUL to each test record
    test_data <- test_data %>%
      left_join(test_rul, by = "unit_number") %>%
      mutate(RUL = true_RUL - (max_time - time_in_cycles)) %>%
      select(-true_RUL, -max_time)
  }
  
  return(test_data)
}

# Apply preparation to all training datasets
prepared_train_data <- list()
for (i in 1:length(file_ids)) {
  file_id <- file_ids[i]
  train_key <- paste0("train_", file_id)
  
  if (train_key %in% names(data_list)) {
    prepared_train_data[[train_key]] <- prepare_train_data(data_list[[train_key]])
    cat(sprintf("Prepared %s: %d rows, %d columns\n", 
               train_key, nrow(prepared_train_data[[train_key]]), 
               ncol(prepared_train_data[[train_key]])))
  }
}

# Prepare test data with RUL values
prepared_test_data <- list()
for (i in 1:length(file_ids)) {
  file_id <- file_ids[i]
  test_key <- paste0("test_", file_id)
  rul_key <- paste0("RUL_", file_id)
  
  if (test_key %in% names(data_list)) {
    prepared_test_data[[test_key]] <- prepare_test_data(
      data_list[[test_key]], 
      data_list[[rul_key]]
    )
    cat(sprintf("Prepared %s: %d rows, %d columns\n", 
               test_key, nrow(prepared_test_data[[test_key]]), 
               ncol(prepared_test_data[[test_key]])))
  }
}
```

## Enhanced Exploratory Data Analysis

```{r data_exploration}
# Load necessary packages
library(tidyverse)  # This loads dplyr, ggplot2, and other required packages

# Function to identify sensor columns
get_sensor_columns <- function(data) {
  # Identify likely sensor columns (based on your data)
  sensor_patterns <- c("^T", "^P", "^N", "^W", "^phi", "^BPR", "^far", "^ht", "^epr", "^Ps")
  
  all_cols <- colnames(data)
  sensor_cols <- character(0)
  
  # Collect all columns matching patterns
  for (pattern in sensor_patterns) {
    matched_cols <- all_cols[grepl(pattern, all_cols)]
    sensor_cols <- c(sensor_cols, matched_cols)
  }
  
  # Deduplicate and remove any non-existent columns
  sensor_cols <- unique(sensor_cols)
  sensor_cols <- sensor_cols[sensor_cols %in% all_cols]
  
  # Fallback if no columns matched
  if (length(sensor_cols) == 0) {
    # Exclude known non-sensor columns
    non_sensors <- c("unit_number", "time_in_cycles", "RUL", 
                   "setting_1", "setting_2", "setting_3", "max_time")
    sensor_cols <- setdiff(all_cols, non_sensors)
  }
  
  return(sensor_cols)
}

# Analyze sensor distributions and trends
analyze_dataset <- function(dataset, name) {
  # Identify sensor columns
  sensors <- get_sensor_columns(dataset)
  
  # Basic statistics
  cat("\n=== Dataset:", name, "===\n")
  cat("Units:", length(unique(dataset$unit_number)), "\n")
  cat("Total records:", nrow(dataset), "\n")
  
  # Handle potential missing RUL column gracefully
  if ("RUL" %in% colnames(dataset)) {
    cat("RUL range:", min(dataset$RUL, na.rm = TRUE), "to", max(dataset$RUL, na.rm = TRUE), "\n\n")
    
    # Plot RUL distribution
    p1 <- ggplot(dataset, aes(x = RUL)) +
      geom_histogram(bins = 50, fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.7) +
      labs(title = paste("RUL Distribution -", name),
           x = "Remaining Useful Life (cycles)",
           y = "Count") +
      theme_minimal()
    print(p1)
  } else {
    cat("No RUL column found in this dataset\n\n")
  }
  
  # Plot some sensor trends for a sample unit
  # Use slice_max instead of arrange + slice
  sample_unit_df <- dataset %>% 
    group_by(unit_number) %>%
    summarize(cycles = max(time_in_cycles)) %>%
    slice_max(order_by = cycles, n = 1)
  
  sample_unit <- sample_unit_df$unit_number[1]
  
  unit_data <- dataset %>% 
    filter(unit_number == sample_unit) %>%
    select(time_in_cycles, any_of("RUL"), all_of(head(sensors, 6)))
  
  long_unit_data <- unit_data %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = -c(time_in_cycles, any_of("RUL")),
                 names_to = "sensor",
                 values_to = "value")
  
  p2 <- ggplot(long_unit_data, aes(x = time_in_cycles, y = value, color = sensor)) +
    geom_line() +
    labs(title = paste("Sensor Readings for Unit", sample_unit),
         x = "Cycles",
         y = "Sensor Value") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom")
  
  print(p2)
  
  # Calculate correlation with RUL (if RUL exists)
  if ("RUL" %in% colnames(dataset)) {
    sensor_data <- dataset %>% select(all_of(sensors), RUL)
    cor_with_rul <- sapply(sensor_data %>% select(-RUL), function(x) {
      cor(x, sensor_data$RUL, use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
    })
    
    cor_df <- data.frame(
      sensor = names(cor_with_rul),
      correlation = cor_with_rul
    ) %>%
      arrange(desc(abs(correlation)))
    
    # Plot top correlations
    p3 <- ggplot(head(cor_df, 10), 
                aes(x = reorder(sensor, abs(correlation)), y = correlation)) +
      geom_bar(stat = "identity", aes(fill = correlation > 0)) +
      coord_flip() +
      labs(title = "Top Correlations with RUL",
           x = "Sensor",
           y = "Correlation with RUL") +
      theme_minimal() +
      scale_fill_discrete(name = "Direction", labels = c("Negative", "Positive"))
    
    print(p3)
    
    # Return top correlated sensors for later use
    return(head(cor_df$sensor, 10))
  } else {
    cat("Cannot calculate correlations without RUL column\n")
    return(sensors[1:10])  # Return first 10 sensors as fallback
  }
}

# Before analyzing, make sure your prepared_train_data has the RUL column
# Here's how to prepare your data with RUL values:

prepare_train_data <- function(data_list) {
  prepared_data <- list()
  
  for (i in 1:length(file_ids)) {
    file_id <- file_ids[i]
    train_key <- paste0("train_", file_id)
    
    # Skip if data is not available
    if (!train_key %in% names(data_list)) {
      next
    }
    
    # Get the training data for this file ID
    train_data <- data_list[[train_key]]
    
    # Calculate RUL for each unit
    train_data_with_rul <- train_data %>%
      group_by(unit_number) %>%
      mutate(max_time = max(time_in_cycles),
             RUL = max_time - time_in_cycles) %>%
      ungroup()
    
    # Store the prepared data
    prepared_data[[train_key]] <- train_data_with_rul
  }
  
  return(prepared_data)
}

# Prepare the training data
prepared_train_data <- prepare_train_data(data_list)

# Get sensor columns for analysis
if (length(prepared_train_data) > 0) {
  first_dataset <- prepared_train_data[[1]]
  sensor_cols <- get_sensor_columns(first_dataset)
  cat("Identified sensor columns:", paste(sensor_cols, collapse=", "), "\n")
  
  # Analyze first dataset in detail
  top_sensors_fd001 <- analyze_dataset(first_dataset, names(prepared_train_data)[1])
} else {
  cat("No prepared data available for analysis\n")
}
```

## Improved Feature Engineering

```{r feature_engineering}
compute_features <- function(dataset, window_sizes = c(5, 10, 20), lags = c(1, 3, 5)) {
  # Get sensor columns
  sensor_cols <- get_sensor_columns(dataset)
  
  # Process each unit independently for time series integrity
  result <- dataset %>%
    group_by(unit_number) %>%
    arrange(unit_number, time_in_cycles) %>%
    mutate(
      # Normalize each sensor by subtracting mean and dividing by std dev
      across(all_of(sensor_cols), list(
        norm = ~((.x - mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE)) / (sd(.x, na.rm = TRUE) + 1e-8))
      ), .names = "{.col}_norm"),
      
      # Life percentage (normalized cycle)
      life_pct = time_in_cycles / max(time_in_cycles),
      # Time since first observation
      cycles_since_first = time_in_cycles - min(time_in_cycles)
    ) %>%
    ungroup()
  
  # For each window size, calculate rolling features
  for (w in window_sizes) {
    result <- result %>%
      group_by(unit_number) %>%
      arrange(unit_number, time_in_cycles) %>%
      mutate(
        # Rolling window features
        across(all_of(sensor_cols), list(
          # Rolling mean
          mean = ~zoo::rollmean(.x, k = w, fill = NA, align = "right"),
          # Rolling standard deviation - FIXED HERE
          sd = ~zoo::rollapply(.x, width = w, 
                             FUN = function(x) sd(x, na.rm = TRUE), 
                             fill = NA, align = "right"),
          # Rolling slope (trend)
          slope = ~zoo::rollapply(.x, width = w, 
                                FUN = function(x) {
                                  if (length(unique(x)) <= 1 || all(is.na(x))) return(0)
                                  tryCatch({
                                    coef(lm(x ~ seq_along(x)))[2]
                                  }, error = function(e) 0)
                                }, 
                                fill = NA, align = "right")
        ), .names = "{.col}_w{w}_{.fn}")
      ) %>%
      ungroup()
  }
  
  # Add lag features
  for (lag in lags) {
    result <- result %>%
      group_by(unit_number) %>%
      arrange(unit_number, time_in_cycles) %>%
      mutate(
        across(all_of(sensor_cols), list(
          # Lagged values
          lag = ~lag(.x, n = lag, default = NA)
        ), .names = "{.col}_lag{lag}"),
        
        # Differences (current - lagged)
        across(all_of(sensor_cols), list(
          diff = ~.x - lag(.x, n = lag, default = NA)
        ), .names = "{.col}_diff{lag}")
      ) %>%
      ungroup()
  }
  
  # Calculate moving averages relative to historical values
  result <- result %>%
    group_by(unit_number) %>%
    arrange(unit_number, time_in_cycles) %>%
    mutate(
      # For select sensors, calculate ratios to historical means
      across(all_of(head(sensor_cols, min(10, length(sensor_cols)))), list(
        ratio_to_init = ~.x / mean(head(.x, 3), na.rm = TRUE)
      ), .names = "{.col}_ratio")
    ) %>%
    ungroup()
  
  # Handle any infinite or NaN values
  result <- result %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ifelse(is.infinite(.x) | is.nan(.x), NA, .x)))
  
  return(result)
}

# Apply feature engineering to all datasets
cat("Engineering features for datasets...\n")
engineered_train_data <- list()
for (name in names(prepared_train_data)) {
  cat("Processing", name, "...\n")
  engineered_train_data[[name]] <- compute_features(prepared_train_data[[name]])
  cat("  Created", ncol(engineered_train_data[[name]]), "features\n")
}

# Apply the same feature engineering to test datasets
engineered_test_data <- list()
for (name in names(prepared_test_data)) {
  cat("Processing", name, "...\n")
  engineered_test_data[[name]] <- compute_features(prepared_test_data[[name]])
  cat("  Created", ncol(engineered_test_data[[name]]), "features\n")
}

# Show a sample of the engineered features
head(names(engineered_train_data[[1]]), 20)
```

## Feature Selection and Dimensionality Reduction

```{r feature_selection}
# Enhanced feature selection function
select_features <- function(dataset, target_col = "RUL", top_n = 30, 
                          method = "rf", sample_size = 10000) {
  # Ensure we work with a tibble
  dataset <- as_tibble(dataset)
  
  # Handle large datasets by sampling
  if (nrow(dataset) > sample_size) {
    set.seed(42)
    dataset <- dataset %>% sample_n(sample_size)
  }
  
  # Exclude non-feature columns
  exclude_cols <- c("unit_number", "time_in_cycles", target_col)
  feature_cols <- setdiff(names(dataset), exclude_cols)
  
  # Keep only numeric features with variation
  is_numeric <- sapply(dataset[feature_cols], is.numeric)
  feature_cols <- feature_cols[is_numeric]
  
  # Remove near-zero variance features
  var_info <- sapply(dataset[feature_cols], function(x) {
    var(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  })
  feature_cols <- feature_cols[var_info > 1e-8]
  
  # Remove highly correlated features (optional)
  if (length(feature_cols) > 100) {
    cor_matrix <- cor(dataset[feature_cols], use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
    highly_correlated <- findCorrelation(cor_matrix, cutoff = 0.9)
    if (length(highly_correlated) > 0) {
      feature_cols <- feature_cols[-highly_correlated]
    }
  }
  
  # Prepare data for feature selection
  X <- dataset %>% 
    select(all_of(feature_cols)) %>%
    drop_na()
  
  y <- dataset[[target_col]][!is.na(rowSums(as.matrix(X)))]
  
  # Skip feature selection if no valid features
  if (length(feature_cols) == 0 || nrow(X) < 10) {
    warning("Insufficient valid data for feature selection")
    return(character(0))
  }
  
  # Feature selection method
  if (method == "rf") {
    set.seed(42)
    
    tryCatch({
      # Use random forest for feature importance
      cat("Running random forest for feature selection...\n")
      rf_model <- randomForest(x = X, y = y, ntree = 100, importance = TRUE)
      
      # Extract importance scores
      imp <- importance(rf_model)
      imp_df <- data.frame(
        Feature = rownames(imp),
        Importance = imp[, "%IncMSE"]
      )
      
      # Sort by importance
      imp_df <- imp_df[order(imp_df$Importance, decreasing = TRUE), ]
      
      # Select top features
      top_features <- as.character(head(imp_df$Feature, top_n))
      
      return(list(
        features = top_features,
        importance = imp_df
      ))
    }, error = function(e) {
      warning("Error in RF feature selection: ", e$message)
      return(list(
        features = feature_cols[1:min(top_n, length(feature_cols))],
        importance = data.frame(Feature = feature_cols, Importance = 1)
      ))
    })
  } else if (method == "correlation") {
    # Use correlation with target
    cor_values <- sapply(X, function(x) {
      abs(cor(x, y, use = "pairwise.complete.obs"))
    })
    
    # Sort by absolute correlation
    cor_df <- data.frame(
      Feature = names(cor_values),
      Importance = cor_values
    )
    cor_df <- cor_df[order(cor_df$Importance, decreasing = TRUE), ]
    
    # Return top features
    return(list(
      features = as.character(head(cor_df$Feature, top_n)),
      importance = cor_df
    ))
  }
}

# Select features for each dataset
selected_features <- list()
feature_importance <- list()

for (ds_name in names(engineered_train_data)) {
  cat("Selecting features for", ds_name, "...\n")
  result <- select_features(engineered_train_data[[ds_name]], top_n = 30)
  
  selected_features[[ds_name]] <- result$features
  feature_importance[[ds_name]] <- result$importance
  
  cat("  Selected", length(selected_features[[ds_name]]), "features\n")
  cat("  Top 5:", paste(head(selected_features[[ds_name]], 5), collapse=", "), "\n")
  
  # Visualize feature importance
  ggplot(head(feature_importance[[ds_name]], 15), 
        aes(x = reorder(Feature, Importance), y = Importance)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
    coord_flip() +
    labs(title = paste("Top Features -", ds_name),
         x = "Feature",
         y = "Importance") +
    theme_minimal()
}
```

## Model Training and Evaluation Framework

```{r model_framework}
# Prepare data for modeling
prepare_model_data <- function(dataset, features, id_vars = c("unit_number", "time_in_cycles", "RUL")) {
  # Make sure features are in the dataset
  features <- features[features %in% colnames(dataset)]
  
  # If no features, return NULL
  if (length(features) == 0) {
    warning("No valid features provided")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Prepare data with features and targets
  model_data <- dataset %>%
    select(all_of(c(id_vars, features))) %>%
    drop_na()
  
  return(model_data)
}

# Split data by units to avoid data leakage
split_by_units <- function(data, train_pct = 0.7) {
  units <- unique(data$unit_number)
  n_units <- length(units)
  
  # Shuffle and split
  set.seed(42)
  train_units <- sample(units, size = floor(train_pct * n_units))
  
  # Create training and validation sets
  train_data <- data %>% filter(unit_number %in% train_units)
  val_data <- data %>% filter(!(unit_number %in% train_units))
  
  return(list(
    train = train_data,
    validation = val_data
  ))
}

# Custom RMSE scoring function
rmse_score <- function(actual, predicted) {
  sqrt(mean((actual - predicted)^2))
}

# Custom RMSE-based assessment for CMAPSS data
custom_rmse <- function(actual, predicted) {
  d <- actual - predicted
  
  # Penalizing early predictions more than late predictions
  score <- ifelse(d >= 0, 
                exp(d/13) - 1,    # Late prediction: exp(d/13) - 1
                exp(-d/10) - 1)   # Early prediction: exp(-d/10) - 1
  
  return(mean(score))
}

# Enhanced model evaluation function
evaluate_model <- function(model, X_test, y_test, model_type) {
  # Make predictions
  if (model_type == "rf") {
    y_pred <- predict(model, X_test)
  } else if (model_type == "xgb") {
    y_pred <- predict(model, as.matrix(X_test))
  } else if (model_type == "gbm") {
    y_pred <- predict(model, as.data.frame(X_test), n.trees = model$n.trees)
  } else if (model_type == "elastic_net") {
    y_pred <- predict(model, as.matrix(X_test), s = "lambda.min")[,1]
  } else if (model_type == "lightgbm") {
    y_pred <- predict(model, as.matrix(X_test))
  } else {
    stop("Unknown model type: ", model_type)
  }
  
  # Calculate metrics
  metrics <- list(
    RMSE = rmse_score(y_test, y_pred),
    MAE = mean(abs(y_test - y_pred)),
    CMAPSS_Score = custom_rmse(y_test, y_pred),
    R2 = 1 - sum((y_test - y_pred)^2) / sum((y_test - mean(y_test))^2)
  )
  
  return(list(
    metrics = metrics,
    predictions = y_pred
  ))
}
```

```{r}
extract_frequency_features <- function(dataset, sensor_cols) {
  result <- dataset %>%
    group_by(unit_number) %>%
    arrange(time_in_cycles) %>%
    do({
      unit_data <- .
      
      # Apply FFT to each sensor
      fft_features <- lapply(sensor_cols, function(col) {
        # Get the sensor data
        signal <- unit_data[[col]]
        
        # Apply FFT
        fft_result <- fft(signal)
        
        # Extract magnitude
        magnitude <- Mod(fft_result)[1:(length(fft_result)/2)]
        
        # Get dominant frequencies
        sorted_mag <- sort(magnitude, decreasing = TRUE)
        top_5_freq <- sorted_mag[1:min(5, length(sorted_mag))]
        
        # Calculate summary statistics of the frequency spectrum
        data.frame(
          mean_magnitude = mean(magnitude),
          max_magnitude = max(magnitude),
          sum_top5_magnitude = sum(top_5_freq),
          spectral_kurtosis = kurtosis(magnitude),
          spectral_entropy = -sum(magnitude * log(magnitude + 1e-10)) / log(length(magnitude))
        )
      })
      
      # Combine all features into a single data frame
      fft_df <- do.call(cbind, fft_features)
      colnames(fft_df) <- paste0(rep(sensor_cols, each = 5), 
                                "_fft_", 
                                c("mean", "max", "top5sum", "kurtosis", "entropy"))
      
      # Add the original unit data
      cbind(unit_data, fft_df)
    }) %>%
    ungroup()
  
  return(result)
}
```

```{r}
extract_wavelet_features <- function(dataset, sensor_cols, wavelet = "haar", level = 3) {
  result <- dataset %>%
    group_by(unit_number) %>%
    arrange(time_in_cycles) %>%
    do({
      unit_data <- .
      
      # Apply wavelet transform to each sensor
      wavelet_features <- lapply(sensor_cols, function(col) {
        # Get the sensor data
        signal <- unit_data[[col]]
        
        # Ensure signal length is sufficient (power of 2 ideally)
        n <- length(signal)
        padded_length <- 2^ceiling(log2(n))
        padded_signal <- c(signal, rep(signal[n], padded_length - n))
        
        # Apply wavelet transform
        wt <- dwt(padded_signal, wf = wavelet, n.levels = level)
        
        # Extract features from each level
        wavelet_stats <- sapply(1:level, function(l) {
          coeffs <- accessD(wt, level = l)
          c(mean(abs(coeffs)), sd(coeffs), 
            quantile(coeffs, probs = c(0.25, 0.75)),
            sum(coeffs^2))
        })
        
        # Reshape into a data frame
        wavelet_df <- as.data.frame(t(wavelet_stats))
        colnames(wavelet_df) <- c("mean", "sd", "q25", "q75", "energy")
        
        # Create column names with sensor and level
        result_df <- data.frame(matrix(as.numeric(t(wavelet_df)), nrow = 1))
        colnames(result_df) <- paste0(col, "_wavelet_L", rep(1:level, each = 5), 
                                    "_", rep(c("mean", "sd", "q25", "q75", "energy"), level))
        return(result_df)
      })
      
      # Combine all features
      wavelet_df <- do.call(cbind, wavelet_features)
      
      # Add the original unit data
      cbind(unit_data, wavelet_df)
    }) %>%
    ungroup()
  
  return(result)
}
```

```{r}
create_health_indicators <- function(dataset, sensor_cols, n_components = 3) {
  # Extract only sensor data for PCA
  sensor_data <- dataset %>% select(all_of(sensor_cols))
  
  # Scale the data
  scaled_data <- scale(sensor_data)
  
  # Apply PCA
  pca_result <- prcomp(scaled_data, center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)
  
  # Extract the top components
  components <- pca_result$x[, 1:n_components]
  
  # Create health indicators
  health_indicators <- as.data.frame(components)
  colnames(health_indicators) <- paste0("health_indicator_", 1:n_components)
  
  # Add health score - weighted sum of components
  explained_var <- pca_result$sdev^2 / sum(pca_result$sdev^2)
  weights <- explained_var[1:n_components] / sum(explained_var[1:n_components])
  
  # Calculate health score (higher value means worse health)
  health_score <- as.matrix(health_indicators) %*% weights
  
  # Add health indicators to the original dataset
  result <- cbind(dataset, health_indicators, health_score = health_score)
  
  return(list(
    data = result,
    pca_model = pca_result,
    explained_variance = explained_var[1:n_components]
  ))
}
```

```{r}
enhanced_feature_engineering <- function(dataset, sensor_cols) {
  # Original features
  cat("Creating standard time-domain features...\n")
  result <- compute_features(dataset)
  
  # Extract frequency-domain features
  cat("Adding frequency domain features...\n")
  result <- extract_frequency_features(result, sensor_cols)
  
  # Extract wavelet features
  cat("Adding wavelet transform features...\n")
  result <- extract_wavelet_features(result, sensor_cols)
  
  # Create health indicators
  cat("Creating health indicators using PCA...\n")
  hi_result <- create_health_indicators(result, sensor_cols)
  result <- hi_result$data
  
  # Log the overall process
  cat("Total features created:", ncol(result), "\n")
  cat("PCA explained variance:", paste(round(hi_result$explained_variance * 100, 2), "% ", sep=""), "\n")
  
  return(list(
    data = result,
    pca_model = hi_result$pca_model
  ))
}
```

```{r}
prepare_sequence_data <- function(data, features, sequence_length = -1, stride = 1) {
  # If sequence_length is -1, use all available time steps for each unit
  data <- as_tibble(data)
  
  # Determine the features to use
  feature_cols <- features[features %in% colnames(data)]
  
  # Prepare sequences by unit
  sequences <- list()
  labels <- list()
  unit_ids <- list()
  
  # Process each unit
  for (unit in unique(data$unit_number)) {
    # Get data for this unit
    unit_data <- data %>% 
      filter(unit_number == unit) %>%
      arrange(time_in_cycles)
    
    # Extract features and target
    X_unit <- as.matrix(unit_data[, feature_cols])
    y_unit <- as.vector(unit_data$RUL)
    
    # Determine sequence length
    seq_len <- if (sequence_length <= 0) nrow(unit_data) else sequence_length
    
    # Create sequences
    for (i in seq(1, nrow(unit_data) - seq_len + 1, by = stride)) {
      end_idx <- i + seq_len - 1
      if (end_idx <= nrow(unit_data)) {
        sequences[[length(sequences) + 1]] <- X_unit[i:end_idx, ]
        labels[[length(labels) + 1]] <- y_unit[end_idx]
        unit_ids[[length(unit_ids) + 1]] <- unit
      }
    }
  }
  
  # Convert to arrays for Keras
  X <- array(0, dim = c(length(sequences), seq_len, length(feature_cols)))
  y <- numeric(length(sequences))
  units <- numeric(length(sequences))
  
  for (i in 1:length(sequences)) {
    X[i, , ] <- sequences[[i]]
    y[i] <- labels[[i]]
    units[i] <- unit_ids[[i]]
  }
  
  return(list(
    X = X,
    y = y,
    units = units,
    feature_names = feature_cols
  ))
}
```

```{r}
build_lstm_model <- function(input_shape, dropout_rate = 0.2) {
  # Initialize keras
  library(keras)
  
  # Create sequential model
  model <- keras_model_sequential() %>%
    layer_lstm(units = 64, return_sequences = TRUE, input_shape = input_shape) %>%
    layer_dropout(rate = dropout_rate) %>%
    layer_lstm(units = 32, return_sequences = FALSE) %>%
    layer_dropout(rate = dropout_rate) %>%
    layer_dense(units = 16, activation = 'relu') %>%
    layer_dense(units = 1, activation = 'linear')
  
  # Compile model
  model %>% compile(
    loss = 'mean_squared_error',
    optimizer = optimizer_adam(learning_rate = 0.001),
    metrics = c('mae')
  )
  
  return(model)
}
```

```{r}
train_lstm_model <- function(X_train, y_train, X_val, y_val, 
                           batch_size = 32, epochs = 100) {
  # Create callbacks
  callbacks <- list(
    callback_early_stopping(monitor = "val_loss", patience = 10, restore_best_weights = TRUE),
    callback_reduce_lr_on_plateau(monitor = "val_loss", factor = 0.5, patience = 5)
  )
  
  # Train model
  history <- fit(
    lstm_model,
    x = X_train,
    y = y_train,
    batch_size = batch_size,
    epochs = epochs,
    validation_data = list(X_val, y_val),
    callbacks = callbacks,
    verbose = 1
  )
  
  return(history)
}
```

```{r}
prepare_survival_data <- function(data) {
  # Create event indicator (1 = failure occurred, 0 = censored)
  surv_data <- data %>%
    group_by(unit_number) %>%
    mutate(
      # Last observation for each unit is the failure point
      is_failure = time_in_cycles == max(time_in_cycles),
      # Event indicator (1 for failure, 0 for censored)
      event = as.numeric(is_failure),
      # Time to event is the RUL
      time = RUL
    ) %>%
    ungroup()
  
  return(surv_data)
}
```

```{r}
train_survival_forest <- function(data, features, n_trees = 100) {
  library(randomForestSRC)
  
  # Prepare formula
  formula_str <- paste("Surv(time, event) ~", paste(features, collapse = " + "))
  formula_obj <- as.formula(formula_str)
  
  # Train model
  rsf_model <- rfsrc(
    formula = formula_obj,
    data = data,
    ntree = n_trees,
    importance = TRUE
  )
  
  return(rsf_model)
}

# Accelerated Failure Time model
train_aft_model <- function(data, features) {
  library(survival)
  
  # Prepare formula
  formula_str <- paste("Surv(time, event) ~", paste(features, collapse = " + "))
  formula_obj <- as.formula(formula_str)
  
  # Train Weibull AFT model
  aft_model <- survreg(
    formula = formula_obj,
    data = data,
    dist = "weibull"
  )
  
  return(aft_model)
}
```

```{r}
predict_rul_with_intervals <- function(model, newdata, alpha = 0.05) {
  if (class(model)[1] == "rfsrc") {
    # Prediction for Random Survival Forest
    preds <- predict(model, newdata = newdata)
    
    # Extract predicted survival function
    surv_curves <- preds$survival
    times <- model$time.interest
    
    # Calculate median survival time (RUL prediction)
    median_rul <- sapply(1:nrow(surv_curves), function(i) {
      curve <- surv_curves[i,]
      # Find time where survival probability crosses 0.5
      idx <- which(curve <= 0.5)[1]
      if (is.na(idx)) {
        # If no crossing, use max time
        return(max(times))
      } else {
        return(times[idx])
      }
    })
    
    # Calculate prediction intervals
    lower_rul <- sapply(1:nrow(surv_curves), function(i) {
      curve <- surv_curves[i,]
      idx <- which(curve <= (1-alpha/2))[1]
      if (is.na(idx)) return(max(times)) else return(times[idx])
    })
    
    upper_rul <- sapply(1:nrow(surv_curves), function(i) {
      curve <- surv_curves[i,]
      idx <- which(curve <= (alpha/2))[1]
      if (is.na(idx)) return(max(times)) else return(times[idx])
    })
    
  } else if (class(model)[1] == "survreg") {
    # Prediction for AFT model
    preds <- predict(model, newdata = newdata, type = "quantile", 
                    p = c(alpha/2, 0.5, 1-alpha/2))
    
    # Extract predictions and intervals
    lower_rul <- preds[,1]
    median_rul <- preds[,2]
    upper_rul <- preds[,3]
  } else {
    stop("Unsupported model type")
  }
  
  return(data.frame(
    RUL = median_rul,
    lower = lower_rul,
    upper = upper_rul
  ))
}

```

```{r}
train_stacked_ensemble <- function(train_data, features, target = "RUL") {
  # Initialize H2O
  h2o.init()
  
  # Convert data to H2O frames
  h2o_train <- as.h2o(train_data)
  
  # Define features and target
  x <- features
  y <- target
  
  # Train base models
  cat("Training base models for ensemble...\n")
  
  # Random Forest
  rf_model <- h2o.randomForest(
    x = x,
    y = y,
    training_frame = h2o_train,
    ntrees = 100,
    max_depth = 20,
    seed = 42
  )
  
  # Gradient Boosting Machine
  gbm_model <- h2o.gbm(
    x = x,
    y = y,
    training_frame = h2o_train,
    ntrees = 100,
    max_depth = 10,
    learn_rate = 0.05,
    seed = 42
  )
  
  # XGBoost
  xgb_model <- h2o.xgboost(
    x = x,
    y = y,
    training_frame = h2o_train,
    ntrees = 100,
    max_depth = 10,
    learn_rate = 0.05,
    seed = 42
  )
  
  # Deep Learning
  dl_model <- h2o.deeplearning(
    x = x,
    y = y,
    training_frame = h2o_train,
    hidden = c(64, 32, 16),
    epochs = 50,
    seed = 42
  )
  
  # Train a stacked ensemble
  cat("Training stacked ensemble...\n")
  ensemble <- h2o.stackedEnsemble(
    x = x,
    y = y,
    training_frame = h2o_train,
    base_models = list(rf_model, gbm_model, xgb_model, dl_model)
  )
  
  # Return all models
  return(list(
    ensemble = ensemble,
    base_models = list(
      rf = rf_model,
      gbm = gbm_model,
      xgb = xgb_model,
      dl = dl_model
    )
  ))
}
```

```{r}
weighted_ensemble_predict <- function(models, weights, X_test) {
  # Get predictions from each model
  predictions <- lapply(1:length(models), function(i) {
    model <- models[[i]]
    model_type <- names(models)[i]
    
    if (model_type == "rf") {
      return(predict(model, X_test))
    } else if (model_type == "xgb") {
      return(predict(model, as.matrix(X_test)))
    } else if (model_type == "gbm") {
      return(predict(model, as.data.frame(X_test), n.trees = model$n.trees))
    } else if (model_type == "lstm") {
      return(as.vector(predict(model, X_test)))
    } else {
      stop("Unsupported model type:", model_type)
    }
  })
  
  # Weight predictions
  weighted_preds <- matrix(0, nrow = nrow(X_test), ncol = 1)
  for (i in 1:length(predictions)) {
    weighted_preds <- weighted_preds + weights[i] * predictions[[i]]
  }
  
  return(weighted_preds)
}
```

```{r}
# SHAP values for feature importance
calculate_shap_values <- function(model, X, model_type) {
  library(shapviz)
  
  if (model_type == "xgb") {
    # For XGBoost models
    shap <- shapviz(model, X_pred = as.matrix(X))
  } else if (model_type == "rf") {
    # For Random Forest models
    pred <- predict(model, X, nodes = TRUE)
    shap <- shapviz(model, X_pred = X, pred_fun = function(m, X) predict(m, X))
  } else {
    stop("Unsupported model type for SHAP calculation")
  }
  
  return(shap)
}

# Plot SHAP values
plot_shap_summary <- function(shap_values) {
  sv_importance(shap_values)
}

```

```{r}
# LIME explanation for individual predictions
explain_prediction_with_lime <- function(model, X_train, X_test, model_type, instance_idx = 1) {
  library(lime)
  
  # Create LIME explainer
  if (model_type == "rf") {
    explainer <- lime(X_train, model, n_bins = 10)
    explanation <- explain(X_test[instance_idx, , drop = FALSE], explainer, n_features = 10)
  } else if (model_type == "xgb") {
    explainer <- lime(as.data.frame(X_train), model, n_bins = 10)
    explanation <- explain(as.data.frame(X_test[instance_idx, , drop = FALSE]), 
                          explainer, n_features = 10)
  } else {
    stop("Unsupported model type for LIME explanation")
  }
  
  return(explanation)
}

# Plot LIME explanation
plot_lime_explanation <- function(explanation) {
  plot_features(explanation)
}
```

```{r}
# Feature importance across degradation phases
analyze_degradation_phases <- function(data, model, features, model_type, 
                                     phase_breaks = c(0.7, 0.3)) {
  # Define degradation phases based on RUL
  data$phase <- cut(data$RUL / max(data$RUL, na.rm = TRUE),
                  breaks = c(0, phase_breaks, 1),
                  labels = c("End-of-life", "Mid-life", "Early-life"),
                  include.lowest = TRUE)
  
  # Analyze feature importance for each phase
  phase_importance <- list()
  
  for (ph in levels(data$phase)) {
    phase_data <- data %>% filter(phase == ph)
    
    # Skip if not enough data
    if (nrow(phase_data) < 20) next
    
    # Calculate feature importance based on model type
    if (model_type == "rf") {
      # For random forest - retrain on this phase
      rf_phase <- randomForest(
        x = phase_data[, features],
        y = phase_data$RUL,
        ntree = 50,
        importance = TRUE
      )
      importance <- importance(rf_phase)[, "%IncMSE"]
      names(importance) <- features
      
    } else if (model_type == "xgb") {
      # For XGBoost - calculate SHAP values for this phase
      shap <- calculate_shap_values(
        model, 
        as.matrix(phase_data[, features]), 
        "xgb"
      )
      importance <- sv_importance(shap, kind = "beeswarm", 
                                plot = FALSE, return_values = TRUE)
      importance <- setNames(importance$mean_abs_shap, importance$feature)
    }
    
    phase_importance[[ph]] <- importance
  }
  
  # Combine into a single data frame
  importance_df <- do.call(cbind, phase_importance)
  colnames(importance_df) <- names(phase_importance)
  importance_df <- as.data.frame(importance_df)
  importance_df$feature <- rownames(importance_df)
  
  # Reshape for plotting
  importance_long <- importance_df %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = -feature, names_to = "phase", values_to = "importance")
  
  # Plot
  p <- ggplot(importance_long, aes(x = reorder(feature, importance), 
                                  y = importance, fill = phase)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
    coord_flip() +
    labs(title = "Feature Importance Across Degradation Phases",
         x = "Feature",
         y = "Importance") +
    theme_minimal()
  
  return(list(
    plot = p,
    data = importance_long,
    raw_importance = phase_importance
  ))
}

```

```{r}
# Function to update models with new data (online learning)
update_model_online <- function(model, new_data, features, target = "RUL", model_type) {
  if (model_type == "rf") {
    # For Random Forest - retrain with combined data
    updated_model <- randomForest(
      x = new_data[, features],
      y = new_data[[target]],
      ntree = 100,
      mtry = sqrt(length(features)),
      importance = TRUE
    )
    return(updated_model)
    
  } else if (model_type == "xgb") {
    # For XGBoost - update existing model
    dtrain <- xgb.DMatrix(
      data = as.matrix(new_data[, features]),
      label = new_data[[target]]
    )
    
    # Update with 5 more iterations
    updated_model <- xgb.train(
      params = list(
        objective = "reg:squarederror",
        eta = 0.03
      ),
      data = dtrain,
      nrounds = 5,
      xgb_model = model  # Use existing model as starting point
    )
    return(updated_model)
  } else {
    stop("Unsupported model type for online update")
  }
}
```

```{r}
# Assumes FD001-FD004 represent different operating conditions
transfer_learning <- function(source_data, target_data, features, source_model, model_type) {
  # Extract features from source and target
  X_source <- source_data[, features]
  y_source <- source_data$RUL
  
  X_target <- target_data[, features]
  y_target <- target_data$RUL
  
  if (model_type == "xgb") {
    # For XGBoost - use source model as base, fine-tune on target
    dtrain <- xgb.DMatrix(data = as.matrix(X_target), label = y_target)
    
    # Fine-tune with a smaller learning rate
    xgb_transfer <- xgb.train(
      params = list(
        objective = "reg:squarederror",
        eta = 0.01,  # Smaller learning rate for fine-tuning
        max_depth = 6
      ),
      data = dtrain,
      nrounds = 50,
      xgb_model = source_model  # Use source model as starting point
    )
    return(xgb_transfer)
    
  } else if (model_type == "rf") {
    # For RF - use source importance to guide feature selection for target
    importance <- importance(source_model)[, "%IncMSE"]
    top_features <- names(sort(importance, decreasing = TRUE)[1:min(15, length(importance))])
    
    # Train on target with selected features
    rf_transfer <- randomForest(
      x = X_target[, intersect(top_features, colnames(X_target))],
      y = y_target,
      ntree = 100,
      importance = TRUE
    )
    return(rf_transfer)
  } else {
    stop("Unsupported model type for transfer learning")
  }
}

```

```{r}
# Enhanced evaluation metrics
evaluate_rul_prediction <- function(actual, predicted, predicted_intervals = NULL) {
  # Basic metrics
  rmse <- sqrt(mean((actual - predicted)^2))
  mae <- mean(abs(actual - predicted))
  
  # NASA CMAPSS score
  d <- actual - predicted
  score <- ifelse(d >= 0, 
                exp(d/13) - 1,    # Late prediction (underestimation)
                exp(-d/10) - 1)   # Early prediction (overestimation)
  cmapss_score <- mean(score)
  
  # Additional metrics
  mape <- mean(abs((actual - predicted) / actual)) * 100
  
  # Calculate coverage if intervals are provided
  coverage <- NA
  mean_interval_width <- NA
  
  if (!is.null(predicted_intervals)) {
    in_interval <- predicted_intervals$lower <= actual & 
                   actual <= predicted_intervals$upper
    coverage <- mean(in_interval)
    mean_interval_width <- mean(predicted_intervals$upper - predicted_intervals$lower)
  }
  
  # Return metrics
  metrics <- list(
    RMSE = rmse,
    MAE = mae,
    MAPE = mape,
    CMAPSS_Score = cmapss_score,
    Coverage = coverage,
    Mean_Interval_Width = mean_interval_width
  )
  
  return(metrics)
}
```

```{r}
# Compare sensor trends across multiple units
plot_degradation <- function(data, sensors, n_units=3) {
  sample_units <- data %>% 
    group_by(unit_number) %>% 
    filter(max(time_in_cycles) > quantile(data$time_in_cycles, 0.9)) %>% 
    slice_sample(n=n_units) %>% 
    pull(unit_number) %>% unique()

  data %>%
    filter(unit_number %in% sample_units) %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = all_of(sensors), names_to = "sensor") %>%
    ggplot(aes(x=time_in_cycles, y=value, color=as.factor(unit_number))) +
    geom_line(alpha=0.8) +
    facet_wrap(~sensor, scales="free_y", ncol=2) +
    labs(title="Sensor Degradation Paths Across Units",
         x="Time in Cycles", 
         y="Sensor Value",
         color="Unit Number") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(legend.position="bottom")
}

# Usage: plot_degradation(prepared_train_data$train_FD001, top_sensors_fd001)

```

```{r}
# Kaplan-Meier survival curves
library(survminer)

plot_survival_curves <- function(data) {
  fit <- survfit(Surv(RUL) ~ 1, data=data)
  ggsurvplot(fit, data=data, risk.table=TRUE,
             title="Kaplan-Meier Survival Curve",
             xlab="Remaining Useful Life (Cycles)",
             ylab="Survival Probability",
             palette="#E7B800", 
             ggtheme=theme_minimal())
}

# Add to EDA section

```

```{r}
# Model-agnostic feature importance
plot_feature_importance <- function(model, test_data, top_n=15) {
  imp <- varImp(model)$importance
  imp$Feature <- rownames(imp)
  
  imp %>%
    arrange(desc(Overall)) %>%
    head(top_n) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x=reorder(Feature, Overall), y=Overall)) +
    geom_col(fill="steelblue", alpha=0.8) +
    coord_flip() +
    labs(title="Feature Importance Ranking",
         x="Features",
         y="Importance Score") +
    theme_minimal()
}

# Use after model training

```

```{r}
# Explain individual predictions
plot_shap_analysis <- function(model, sample_data) {
  explainer <- shapviz(model, X_pred = data.matrix(sample_data))
  sv_importance(explainer, kind="beehive", max_display=10) +
    labs(title="SHAP Feature Importance",
         x="Mean |SHAP Value|") +
    theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size=9))
  
  sv_dependence(explainer, v="T50", color_var="auto") +
    labs(title="SHAP Dependence Plot for T50")
}

# Requires trained model and sample data

```

```{r}
# Model diagnostic plots
plot_residual_analysis <- function(predictions, actuals) {
  residuals <- actuals - predictions
  p1 <- ggplot() + 
    geom_histogram(aes(residuals), bins=30, fill="steelblue") +
    labs(title="Residual Distribution", x="Residuals")
  
  p2 <- ggplot() +
    geom_point(aes(x=predictions, y=residuals), alpha=0.5) +
    geom_hline(yintercept=0, color="red") +
    labs(title="Residuals vs Predictions", x="Predicted RUL", y="Residuals")
  
  p1 + p2 + plot_layout(ncol=2)
}

# Use after model predictions

```

```{r}
library(plotly)
plot_3d_trajectory <- function(unit_data) {
  plot_ly(unit_data, x=~T50, y=~P15, z=~Nf, 
          color=~RUL, type="scatter3d", mode="lines",
          line=list(width=4, colorscale="Viridis")) %>%
    layout(scene=list(xaxis=list(title="T50"),
                      yaxis=list(title="P15"),
                      zaxis=list(title="Nf")),
           title="3D Sensor Trajectory with RUL Coloring")
}

# For R Markdown output, include:
# htmltools::tagList(plot_3d_trajectory(sample_unit_data))

```

```{r}
# Combined operational view
plot_operational_status <- function(unit_data) {
  p1 <- ggplot(unit_data, aes(x=time_in_cycles, y=RUL)) +
    geom_line(color="darkred") +
    geom_hline(yintercept=30, linetype="dashed") +
    annotate("text", x=mean(unit_data$time_in_cycles), y=35, 
             label="Warning Threshold", color="darkred") +
    labs(title="RUL Projection", x="Cycles", y="Remaining Life")
  
  p2 <- unit_data %>%
    select(time_in_cycles, T50, P15, Nf) %>%
    pivot_longer(-time_in_cycles) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x=time_in_cycles, y=value)) +
    geom_line(color="steelblue") +
    facet_wrap(~name, scales="free_y", ncol=1) +
    labs(x="Cycles", y="Sensor Value")
  
  p1 / p2 + plot_layout(heights=c(1,2))
}

```

```{r}
# Enhanced visualization of results
visualize_rul_prediction <- function(actual, predicted, predicted_intervals = NULL, unit_ids = NULL) {
  # Create data frame for plotting
  results_df <- data.frame(
    Actual = actual,
    Predicted = predicted
  )
  
  if (!is.null(unit_ids)) {
    results_df$Unit <- unit_ids
  } else {
    results_df$Unit <- 1:nrow(results_df)
  }
  
  if (!is.null(predicted_intervals)) {
    results_df$Lower <- predicted_intervals$lower
    results_df$Upper <- predicted_intervals$upper
  }
  
  # Predictions vs Actual
  p1 <- ggplot(results_df, aes(x = Actual, y = Predicted)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
    geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
    labs(title = "Predicted vs Actual RUL",
         x = "Actual RUL",
         y = "Predicted RUL") +
    theme_minimal()
  
  # Error distribution
  results_df$Error <- results_df$Predicted - results_df$Actual
  
  p2 <- ggplot(results_df, aes(x = Error)) +
    geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.7) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
    labs(title = "Prediction Error Distribution",
         x = "Prediction Error (Predicted - Actual)",
         y = "Count") +
    theme_minimal()
  
  # Optional: Include prediction intervals if provided
  if (!is.null(predicted_intervals)) {
    p1 <- p1 +
      geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = Lower, ymax = Upper), width = 0.2, alpha = 0.4, color = "darkgray")
  }

  # Combine the plots
  if ("patchwork" %in% .packages()) {
    return(p1 + p2)
  } else if ("gridExtra" %in% .packages()) {
    gridExtra::grid.arrange(p1, p2, ncol = 2)
  } else {
    print("Install 'patchwork' or 'gridExtra' for plot combining.")
    return(list(p1, p2))
  }
}

```

```{r}
# Correlation heatmap for sensor columns
sensor_cols <- get_sensor_columns(first_dataset)
corr_matrix <- cor(first_dataset %>% select(all_of(sensor_cols)), use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
corrplot::corrplot(corr_matrix, method = "color", type = "lower", tl.cex=0.7)

```

```{r}
# Example: Boxplot of a rolling mean feature vs. RUL bins
first_eng <- engineered_train_data[[1]]
first_eng <- first_eng %>% mutate(RUL_bin = cut(RUL, breaks=5))
ggplot(first_eng, aes(x=RUL_bin, y=T24_w5_mean)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill="skyblue") +
  labs(title="Rolling Mean of T24 vs. RUL Bin", x="RUL Bin", y="T24 (5-cycle mean)")

```

```{r}
library(caret)
set.seed(123)
# Prepare data (using FD001 as example)
train_df <- engineered_train_data[[1]] %>%
  drop_na() %>%
  select(-unit_number, -time_in_cycles) # Remove non-features

# Split into train/test for demonstration
idx <- sample(seq_len(nrow(train_df)), size = 0.8*nrow(train_df))
train_set <- train_df[idx, ]
test_set <- train_df[-idx, ]

# Train Random Forest
rf_model <- randomForest(RUL ~ ., data = train_set, ntree=100, importance=TRUE)
rf_pred <- predict(rf_model, test_set)
rf_rmse <- sqrt(mean((rf_pred - test_set$RUL)^2))
cat("Random Forest RMSE:", rf_rmse, "\n")

```

```{r}
varImpPlot(rf_model, main="Random Forest Feature Importance")
```

```{r}
library(xgboost)
# Prepare matrices
X_train <- as.matrix(train_set %>% select(-RUL))
y_train <- train_set$RUL
X_test <- as.matrix(test_set %>% select(-RUL))
y_test <- test_set$RUL

xgb_model <- xgboost(data = X_train, label = y_train, nrounds = 100, objective = "reg:squarederror", verbose=0)
xgb_pred <- predict(xgb_model, X_test)
xgb_rmse <- sqrt(mean((xgb_pred - y_test)^2))
cat("XGBoost RMSE:", xgb_rmse, "\n")

```

```{r}
library(caret)
library(rpart)
library(rpart.plot)
library(keras)
library(tensorflow)
library(dplyr)

## Linear Regression
set.seed(123)

# Train Linear Regression
lm_model <- lm(RUL ~ ., data = train_set)
lm_pred <- predict(lm_model, test_set)
lm_rmse <- sqrt(mean((lm_pred - test_set$RUL)^2))
cat("Linear Regression RMSE:", lm_rmse, "\n")

# Quick summary of the linear model
summary_lm <- summary(lm_model)
print(summary_lm$r.squared)

```

```{r}
set.seed(123)

# Train Decision Tree
dt_model <- rpart(RUL ~ ., data = train_set, method = "anova")
dt_pred <- predict(dt_model, test_set)
dt_rmse <- sqrt(mean((dt_pred - test_set$RUL)^2))
cat("Decision Tree RMSE:", dt_rmse, "\n")

# Plot the decision tree
rpart.plot(dt_model, main = "Decision Tree for RUL Prediction")
```

```{r}
## Support Vector Machine (SVM)
set.seed(123)

# Normalize data for SVM (SVMs perform better with normalized data)
preprocess_params <- preProcess(train_set %>% select(-RUL), method = c("center", "scale"))
train_svm <- train_set
train_svm[, names(train_svm) != "RUL"] <- predict(preprocess_params, train_set %>% select(-RUL))
test_svm <- test_set
test_svm[, names(test_svm) != "RUL"] <- predict(preprocess_params, test_set %>% select(-RUL))

# Train SVM model with radial kernel
# Using a smaller subset for training due to computational complexity if dataset is large
if(nrow(train_svm) > 1000) {
  train_idx <- sample(1:nrow(train_svm), 1000)
  svm_train <- train_svm[train_idx,]
} else {
  svm_train <- train_svm
}

# Train the SVM model
svm_model <- svm(RUL ~ ., data = svm_train, 
                kernel = "radial", 
                cost = 10,
                gamma = 0.1)

# Make predictions
svm_pred <- predict(svm_model, test_svm)
svm_rmse <- sqrt(mean((svm_pred - test_svm$RUL)^2))
cat("SVM RMSE:", svm_rmse, "\n")

# Plot the SVM model results
plot(svm_pred ~ test_svm$RUL, 
     main = "SVM Predictions vs Actual",
     xlab = "Actual RUL", 
     ylab = "Predicted RUL")
abline(0, 1, col = "red", lwd = 2)
```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```
